# UC-21.8.14 · Asterisk Voicemail Storage Growth and MWI NOTIFY Failures

## Description

Voicemail stores grow with retained compliance recordings; simultaneous MWI NOTIFY failures strand users without blinking lamps. Combining grep-friendly Asterisk logs with optional disk-usage metrics exposes both logical and physical capacity risk.

## Value

Avoids voicemail outages during revenue-critical periods by alerting on storage pressure and MWI errors before spool partitions fill.

## Implementation

Forward Asterisk logs; tag voicemail-related lines. Alert when voicemail/MWI error strings exceed 10 per hour per mailbox. Separately schedule `index=os sourcetype=asterisk:voicemail_du` from `du -sk /var/spool/asterisk/voicemail` and alert when `voicemail_kb/1024/1024 > 50` (tune per disk). Integrate with retention jobs documented in runbooks.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Separate partition monitoring if voicemail is on dedicated LVM.
• Legal hold process before auto-purge.

Step 1 — Normalize mailbox field to `extension@context`.

Step 2 — Validate MWI errors by testing NOTIFY with `sip set debug` in lab.

Step 3 — Dashboard for top 20 mailboxes by size.

Step 4 — Automate ticket to PBX admin with disk free %.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:full voicemail OR MWI OR Mailbox
| rex field=_raw "(?i)mailbox\s+(?<mailbox>\S+)"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as err_count by _time, host, mailbox
| where err_count > 10
```

## Visualization

Area chart (voicemail_gb), Table (mailboxes with errors), Single value (spool percent full if added).
